conditions: Only applies to new oak. Old (used) oak has depleted its extractable tannin. Magnitude depends on: percentage of new oak, barrel size (smaller = more wood-to-wine surface area = greater extraction), contact time, toast level (heavy toast degrades surface tannins).
explanation: New oak barrels contain ellagitannins (hydrolysable tannins) in the wood cell walls. Ethanol in wine solubilises these compounds, adding wood-derived tannin to the wine's existing grape-derived condensed tannin load. Ellagitannins contribute astringency and also participate in colour stabilisation reactions (polymerisation with anthocyanins). They are chemically distinct from grape condensed tannins and have different sensory and chemical ageing properties.
